axioms.name: Conflict-Agnostic Structure
axioms.statement: Conflict is not the unique driver of narrative; structure can be derived from juxtaposition and resonance.

axioms.name: Perspectival Shift
axioms.statement: Narrative momentum is generated by the radical departure (Ten) rather than causal escalation.

axioms.name: Harmonization Goal
axioms.statement: The goal of the work is the harmonization of seemingly unrelated elements into a unified whole.

levels.name: ACT
levels.description: One of four structural phases: Ki, Sho, Ten, or Ketsu

core_algorithms.name: Perspective-Shift Algorithm
core_algorithms.purpose: Generate narrative momentum through structural juxtaposition
core_algorithms.pseudocode: 1. KI: Establish base reality; 2. SHO: Expand on base; 3. TEN: Introduce independent radical departure; 4. KETSU: Synthesize resonance between Ten and Ki/Sho; RETURN harmony;

diagnostic_questions.question: Does the first act (Ki) establish a clear base reality?
Does the second act (Sho) expand on the base without introducing major conflict?
Is the third act (Ten) a genuine surprise or shift in perspective?
Is the Ten element independent of the prior causal chain?
Does the conclusion (Ketsu) reveal a resonance between the Ten and the base?
Does the whole feel like a unified harmony rather than a resolved conflict?

key_constraints: Ten must be independent of Sho
